SAQ: A 50-year-old man, who was a National level boxing champion presented to the neurology OPD with complains of gradual slowness of movements past 3 months. He has developed tremors in his both hands which he specifically mentions that they appear only on rest. His daughter expresses that he is often complaining of losing balance and frightened of falling. She has noticed that her father's face grows expressionless while speaking and states his muscle movements have become limited due to which he remains depressed often. On examination: He is well oriented to time, place and person. Has slight masked face. Mild dysarthria and tremors in resting left and right hand. Posture is moderate kyphotic and forward head posture. When asked to walk it seems like he is trying the catch the centre of gravity. Motor examination shows limited B/L shoulder flexion and abduction with limited B/L Hip extension. Sensory system and cranial nerves-Intact CVS, RS, Per Abdomen -No Abnormality noted. i.
